region_of_origin: Global (served in Middle Eastern restaurant context in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301116 covers ice cream as a general class. No specific vanilla-or-chocolate variant code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Ice cream and similar frozen desserts
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers ice cream and similar frozen desserts as a category. No flavour-specific sub-code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226888001 covers ice cream as a substance class. No vanilla/chocolate variant code; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard scoop of vanilla or chocolate ice cream weighing approximately 87 g, as typically served in a restaurant or dessert setting.

nutrient_source.title: Ice creams, vanilla (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 19095) and Ice creams, chocolate (FDC ID 19270)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values represent a blended average of USDA SR Legacy vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 19095) and chocolate ice cream (FDC ID 19270) per 100 g, reflecting the dual-flavour nature of this menu item. Values are averaged across both flavours as the record covers either option. USDA SR Legacy is an approved secondary global source under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — ice cream entries; University of Sydney GI Database ice cream records
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for vanilla ice cream in the range of 37–61 (mean approximately 57) and chocolate ice cream similarly in the medium range. A consensus GI of 57 (medium) is applied as a representative value for this dual-flavour item. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 21.3) / 100 ≈ 12.2, where carbohydrate per 87 g serving ≈ 21.3 g.

hypertension.guidance: Ice cream contains approximately 80 mg sodium per 100 g, which is modest; however, individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one scoop and avoid frequent consumption due to the saturated fat and caloric load that may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 21 g of sugar per 100 g and a glycemic index of 57 (medium), ice cream can cause a meaningful postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption to a small single scoop on an occasional basis and monitor blood glucose response carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ice cream provides approximately 6.8 g saturated fat and 44 mg cholesterol per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake to occasional small portions, as regular consumption of saturated fat is associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vanilla or chocolate ice cream is a classic frozen dairy dessert made by churning a mixture of cream, milk, sugar, egg yolks, and flavouring — either vanilla extract or cocoa powder — while freezing it to produce a smooth, creamy, cold confection. The texture is dense and rich, with a melt-in-the-mouth quality derived from the high fat content of the cream base. Vanilla ice cream has a sweet, floral, and subtly aromatic flavour, while chocolate ice cream offers a deeper, bittersweet cocoa-forward taste. Both variants are globally ubiquitous desserts served in restaurants, cafes, and homes across all cultures. In the Singapore context, this item appears on the menu of Sahara Bar & Restaurant at Boat Quay, a Middle Eastern establishment, where it is offered as a dessert scoop alongside shawarma and kebab mains. Nutritionally, a 100 g serving provides approximately 207 kcal, 11 g fat (of which 6.8 g is saturated), 24.5 g carbohydrate, and 21 g sugar. The glycemic index is approximately 57 (medium), making it a moderate glycemic food. Ice cream is a source of calcium (~128 mg/100 g) and vitamin A (~84 mcg RAE/100 g) from its dairy base. Clinically, it is high in sugar and saturated fat, making it a food to be consumed in moderation by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or obesity. It is not suitable for vegans or those with dairy or egg allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a scoop of ice cream?
Is ice cream high in sugar?
What is the GI of ice cream?
Vanilla vs chocolate ice cream nutrition
Ice cream serving size calories Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from USDA SR Legacy data for vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 19095) and chocolate ice cream (FDC ID 19270), averaged to represent this dual-flavour menu item. No Singapore-specific or HPB Foodo entry exists for this exact item. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database records for ice cream, which report a range; a representative mean of 57 is used. Confidence is medium because the source match is adapted/averaged rather than exact.
notes: 1. This record covers a dual-flavour item (vanilla or chocolate ice cream) served as a single scoop at Sahara Bar & Restaurant, Boat Quay, Singapore. Nutrient values are averaged from USDA SR Legacy vanilla ice cream (FDC ID 19095, ~201 kcal/100 g) and chocolate ice cream (FDC ID 19270, ~216 kcal/100 g) to produce a blended representative profile. 2. No HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific item; USDA SR Legacy is used as the approved secondary global source. 3. GI of 57 is a representative estimate from University of Sydney GI Database ice cream records, which report values ranging from 37 to 61 for vanilla and similar ranges for chocolate; the mean is used. 4. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed for this specific restaurant without certification verification. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all populated from USDA SR Legacy averaged values and are not sentinel -1 values. 6. Cuisine tag in the input is 'Middle Eastern' reflecting the restaurant context; the food itself is a globally universal Western dessert and is classified accordingly. 7. The input cuisine tag 'Middle Eastern' is preserved in cuisine_tags context but the food's intrinsic cuisine is classified as Western/International.
